⏺ Here's what this repo is and how it maps to the Alpamayo-R1 paper (arXiv 2511.00088).                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  The paper in one paragraph                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Alpamayo-R1 (a.k.a. "Alpamayo 1") is an NVIDIA Vision-Language-Action model for autonomous driving. It couples Cosmos-Reason (a Physical-AI-pretrained VLM) with a                       
  diffusion/flow-matching trajectory decoder and a Chain-of-Causation (CoC) reasoning trace: the model reads multi-camera video + ego-motion history, emits a causally-linked text         
  rationale, then predicts a 6.4 s / 64-waypoint trajectory at 10 Hz. Training has three stages — pretraining, SFT (to elicit reasoning), and GRPO-style RL (to enforce reasoning↔action   
  consistency and improve reasoning quality). Reported gains: ~12% planning accuracy, −35% close-encounter rate in closed-loop sim, +45% reasoning quality / +37% consistency from RL, ~99
  ms latency.

  Repo layout

  src/alpamayo_r1/ — the released model                                                                                                                                                    
  
  - models/alpamayo_r1.py — AlpamayoR1 (ReasoningVLA with an expert decoder): VLM autoregressively generates CoC text + discrete trajectory tokens, then an expert transformer conditioned 
  on the VLM's KV-cache denoises continuous actions via sample_trajectories_from_data_with_vlm_rollout. ExpertLogitsProcessor masks discrete traj tokens during text generation.
  - models/base_model.py — ReasoningVLA / ReasoningVLAConfig. Wraps a Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration backbone, adds a special-token vocabulary for <|traj_history|>, <|traj_future|>,     
  <|cot_start/end|>, <|meta_action_start/end|>, <|route_*|>, plus <i0>..<i{V-1}> discrete trajectory tokens. TrajectoryFusionMixin.fuse_traj_tokens splices tokenized ego history into the 
  prompt.
  - action_space/ — UnicycleAccelCurvatureActionSpace converts (xyz, rotation) ↔ (acceleration, curvature) with Tikhonov-smoothed kinematic solves; DiscreteTrajectoryTokenizer bins       
  actions into traj_vocab_size tokens; DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer is used for history tokens.                                                                                                
  - diffusion/flow_matching.py — conditional flow matching: beta-schedule noise, Euler integrator, MSE loss against velocity field x - noise — this is the "action expert" loss.
  - models/action_in_proj.py — PerWaypointActionInProjV2: Fourier-encodes each action dim + timestep, MLPs up to the expert's hidden size.                                                 
  - processor/, chat_template/, data/pai.py — Qwen3-VL processor, Physical AI AV dataset loader.                                                                                           
  - test_inference.py — end-to-end demo against one clip, computes minADE.                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  finetune/sft/ — Stage-1 and Stage-2 SFT                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  - train_hf.py + trainer.py — Hydra-configured HuggingFace Trainer with per-prefix LR multipliers (vision encoder can run at 0.1× the LM LR).                                             
  - models/sft_base_model.py — TrainableReasoningVLA. Fuses history + future traj tokens, splits cross-entropy loss into future_traj and others (text/CoC). Stage 1 trains the full VLM on
  discrete trajectory tokens + CoC text (configs/sft_stage1.yaml, DeepSpeed ZeRO-2, vlm.model.visual at 0.1× LR).                                                                          
  - models/sft_alpamayo_r1.py — TrainableAlpamayoR1. Stage 2 freezes the Stage-1 VLM, reads its KV-cache up to <|traj_future_start|>, runs the expert over projected noisy actions, and
  trains only the action expert + in/out projections with flow-matching MSE (configs/sft_stage2.yaml).                                                                                     
  - evaluate_hf.py — accelerate-based eval producing ADE / minADE @ {0.5s, 1s, 3s, 5s}, corner distance.
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  finetune/rl/ — GRPO post-training via Cosmos-RL                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  - models/reasoning_vla/alpamayo_cosmos_rl_post_training_entry.py — registers ReasoningVLA with vLLM and assembles a ModelSpec (cosmos wrapper, weight mapper, data packer, reward fn,    
  Hydra config).                                                  
  - models/reasoning_vla/{rollout.py, trainer.py, vllm_wrapper.py, weight_mapper.py, data_packer.py} — vLLM rollout engine, GRPO trainer (ReasoningVLAGRPOTrainer), DTensor ↔ vLLM weight  
  sync.                                                                                                                                                                                    
  - rewards/aggregated_reward.py — gated reward: if ADE > 3 m → −1; otherwise -w_l2 · (ADE/3) + w_comfort · (comfort − 1). comfort_reward.py scores longitudinal/lateral acceleration,
  jerk, yaw rate/accel against fixed comfort bounds. Only the VLM (text + discrete traj tokens) is RL-trained; the action expert is not updated by RL in this release.                     
  - toml/alpamayo_rvla_rl_local_test.toml — GRPO config: n_generation=12, batch=2, epsilon_low/high=0.2/0.28, kl_beta=0, AdamW lr 2e-6, bf16, FSDP shard-size 4 (local) / 8 (cluster).
  - prefetch/ — node-local shared-memory prefetch server (big speedup: 44 s → 5 s/step claimed).                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  scripts/                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  - download_pai.py — snapshot-downloads the PAI dataset (chunks + specific cameras/labels).                                                                                               
  - curate_pai_samples.py — subsets clip_index.parquet to N clips.
  - convert_release_config_to_training.py — maps the released HF checkpoint into a training-ready layout: remaps alpamayo1_5.* → alpamayo_r1.* Hydra targets, rewrites                     
  model_type/architectures, wires in Cosmos-Reason2-8B tokenizer/processor, symlinks safetensors.                                                                                          
  - convert_cosmos_rl_checkpoint.py — merges per-rank Cosmos-RL DTensor shards (model_rank_*.pth) back into sharded *.safetensors + index so you can from_pretrained after RL.             
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Paper ↔ code correspondence                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   
  │                    Paper concept                    │                                                      Where in the code                                                       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ Cosmos-Reason VLM backbone                          │ base_model.py → Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration; RL uses nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-8B processor                                  │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Chain-of-Causation trace                            │ `<                                                                                                                           │   
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ Discrete trajectory tokens                          │ <i0>..<i{V-1}> + DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer (history) and action-space-based discrete tokenizer (future)                       │   
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ Action expert (continuous trajectory)               │ AlpamayoR1.expert (a stripped Qwen text decoder) + PerWaypointActionInProjV2 + FlowMatching loss                             │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ 6.4 s / 64 waypoints @ 10 Hz                        │ num_future_steps=64, time_step=0.1, tokens_per_future_traj=64, UnicycleAccelCurvatureActionSpace(n_waypoints=64, dt=0.1)     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ Stage 1 SFT (elicit reasoning)                      │ finetune/sft/configs/sft_stage1.yaml + TrainableReasoningVLA.forward (split CE on future_traj and others)                    │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ Stage 2 SFT (action expert)                         │ sft_stage2.yaml + TrainableAlpamayoR1.forward (flow-matching MSE on frozen-VLM KV cache)                                     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤   
  │ RL post-training (GRPO, reasoning-action            │ finetune/rl/** via Cosmos-RL; ADE + comfort reward in aggregated_reward.py (reasoning-only reward is noted as a future       │
  │ consistency)                                        │ addition)                                                                                                                    │   
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Ready for follow-ups — e.g., walking through the VLM-expert KV-cache handoff, the flow-matching training loop, the GRPO reward/rollout flow, or how Stage 2 freezes the VLM in practice.

⏺ Short answer: no — you have most of the training scaffolding, but four specific pieces are missing.
                                           
  What the repo gives you
  - Full SFT pipeline (stage 1 VLM + discrete traj tokens/CoC, stage 2 frozen-VLM flow-matching expert): finetune/sft/                                                                     
  - Full RL pipeline (GRPO via Cosmos-RL with ADE + comfort rewards): finetune/rl/                                                                                                         
  - Trajectory tokenizers (DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer, DiscreteTrajectoryTokenizer), action space, action expert, flow-matching — all backbone-agnostic                                      
  - The 10B teacher (nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for generating labels/trajectories                                                                                                            
  - PAI dataset download + loader for images + egomotion                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  What's missing for the 0.5B variant                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  1. Backbone is hardcoded to Qwen3-VL. base_model.py::_initialize_qwenvl3_vlm constructs Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration directly. To use Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B you'd need a parallel init path 
  (Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration / Qwen2_5_VLConfig) and to update position-id handling — sft_alpamayo_r1.py already has a _process_position_ids_qwen2_5_vl helper, so some plumbing
  was anticipated but not wired through.                                                                                                                                                   
  2. Chain-of-Causation text labels are not in the released data loader. PAIDataset and PhysicalAIAVDatasetLocalInterface only read egomotion + cameras; other feature readers log "not
  implemented yet." The RL README says reasoning labels will be released later. Stage 1 SFT needs CoC text to train; without it you'd either (a) skip CoC, or (b) generate traces from the 
  10B teacher yourself.
  3. No distillation code exists. No teacher/student wrappers, no KD/KL loss, no logit-matching — grep for distill|teacher|student|kd_loss|kl_div returns nothing. You'd build this        
  yourself (SFT on 10B-generated CoC + traj tokens is the simplest path; true logit distillation is more work because the action expert is a separate decoder).                            
  4. No public Cosmos-Reason-0.5B. The paper's 0.5B run presumably started from a physical-AI-pretrained Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B that NVIDIA didn't release. You'd start from vanilla
  Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct (no 0.5B official VL checkpoint exists publicly either — Qwen2.5-VL ships at 3B/7B/32B/72B), which is a real gap vs. the paper's setup.                      
                                                                  
  Practical path if you want to try anyway: swap in Qwen2.5-VL-3B, use the 10B teacher to generate CoC + trajectory tokens on PAI clips, run the existing two-stage SFT, then optionally   
  RL. You will not reproduce the paper's 0.5B result exactly — different base, no physical-AI pretrain, self-generated labels.

⏺ Short answer: no — you have most of the training scaffolding, but four specific pieces are missing.

  What the repo gives you
  - Full SFT pipeline (stage 1 VLM + discrete traj tokens/CoC, stage 2 frozen-VLM flow-matching expert): finetune/sft/
  - Full RL pipeline (GRPO via Cosmos-RL with ADE + comfort rewards): finetune/rl/
  - Trajectory tokenizers (DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer, DiscreteTrajectoryTokenizer), action space, action expert, flow-matching — all backbone-agnostic
  - The 10B teacher (nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for generating labels/trajectories
  - PAI dataset download + loader for images + egomotion

  What's missing for the 0.5B variant

  1. Backbone is hardcoded to Qwen3-VL. base_model.py::_initialize_qwenvl3_vlm constructs Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration directly. To use Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B you'd need a parallel init path
  (Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration / Qwen2_5_VLConfig) and to update position-id handling — sft_alpamayo_r1.py already has a _process_position_ids_qwen2_5_vl helper, so some plumbing
  was anticipated but not wired through.
  2. Chain-of-Causation text labels are not in the released data loader. PAIDataset and PhysicalAIAVDatasetLocalInterface only read egomotion + cameras; other feature readers log "not
  implemented yet." The RL README says reasoning labels will be released later. Stage 1 SFT needs CoC text to train; without it you'd either (a) skip CoC, or (b) generate traces from the
  10B teacher yourself.
  3. No distillation code exists. No teacher/student wrappers, no KD/KL loss, no logit-matching — grep for distill|teacher|student|kd_loss|kl_div returns nothing. You'd build this
  yourself (SFT on 10B-generated CoC + traj tokens is the simplest path; true logit distillation is more work because the action expert is a separate decoder).
  4. No public Cosmos-Reason-0.5B. The paper's 0.5B run presumably started from a physical-AI-pretrained Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B that NVIDIA didn't release. You'd start from vanilla
  Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct (no 0.5B official VL checkpoint exists publicly either — Qwen2.5-VL ships at 3B/7B/32B/72B), which is a real gap vs. the paper's setup.

  Practical path if you want to try anyway: swap in Qwen2.5-VL-3B, use the 10B teacher to generate CoC + trajectory tokens on PAI clips, run the existing two-stage SFT, then optionally
  RL. You will not reproduce the paper's 0.5B result exactly — different base, no physical-AI pretrain, self-generated labels.

❯ What about just running the Cosmos-Reason2-2B or 8B model that is released? How much worse would that be and what baselines do they say in the paper?                                    

⏺ Fetch(https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00088)
  ⎿  Received 52.8KB (200 OK)                                                         

⏺ Fetch(https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.00088)                                                                                                                                                  
  ⎿  Received 8.4MB (200 OK)                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)                                                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                  
⏺ Important clarification first: the released "Alpamayo-R1-10B" is already the Cosmos-Reason2-8B backbone (8B VLM + 2B flow-matching expert = 10B total). The repo's RL config confirms    
  this with vlm_name_or_path: nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-8B. So "just running" Cosmos-Reason2-8B is exactly what you already get when you use the released weights — nothing smaller to train.  
                                                                                      
  The only released size variants in the paper are:                                                                                                                                        
  - Alpamayo-R1-10B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B backbone, released)                                                                                                                                 
  - Alpamayo-R1-0.5B (smaller backbone for comparison, also released on HF as a public-benchmark variant)                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Table 10 — the 0.5B vs 10B head-to-head (public PhysicalAI-AV):                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  ┌────────────────────────┬───────┬───────┬────────────────────┐                                                                                                                          
  │         Metric         │ 0.5B  │  10B  │        Gap         │                                                                                                                          
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤                                                                                                                          
  │ minADE₆@6.4s ↓         │ 0.913 │ 0.849 │ 7% worse at 0.5B   │ 
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤
  │ Close-encounter rate ↓ │ 9.0%  │ 4.0%  │ 2.25× more at 0.5B │                                                                                                                          
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤                                                                                                                          
  │ Off-road rate ↓        │ 19.0% │ 16.0% │ 19% worse          │                                                                                                                          
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤                                                                                                                          
  │ AlpaSim score ↑        │ 0.35  │ 0.72  │ ~½ at 0.5B         │ 
  └────────────────────────┴───────┴───────┴────────────────────┘                                                                                                                          
                                                                  
  So the closed-loop safety gap is large — open-loop minADE looks close (7%), but at-fault close encounters and overall AlpaSim score roughly double when you shrink the backbone.         
                                                                  
  Figure 12 — general-purpose VLM scaling (no Cosmos-Reason, same training recipe):                                                                                                        
  - DINOv2 + Qwen2.5-0.5B: minADE@6s = 0.878                      
  - Qwen2.5-VL-3B: 0.819 (−6.7%)                                                                                                                                                           
  - Qwen2.5-VL-7B: 0.777 (−11.5%)                                 
                                 
  Cosmos-Reason pretraining adds on top (§6.5.3 / Table 11): at 7B scale, Cosmos-Reason-7B hits 66.2% on LingoQA vs Qwen2.5-VL-7B's 62.2% and Qwen2-VL-7B's 52.6% — so physical-AI         
  pretraining is worth ~4 points at the same size.                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  About Cosmos-Reason2-2B: NVIDIA hasn't released a 2B variant of Cosmos-Reason2 publicly (only 7B/8B on HF). So your realistic options are:                                               
  1. Use the released 10B as-is (best, free)                      
  2. Train a 0.5B variant — expect roughly the paper's 0.5B numbers (0.913 minADE, 9% close-encounter rate)                                                                                
  3. There's no middle tier you can reach without training Cosmos-Reason yourself                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Baselines the paper reports against are internal trajectory-only / meta-action-only variants at matched size, not external models like EMMA/DriveVLM — they cite those in related work   
  but don't run head-to-head. The headline numbers (12% planning accuracy gain, 35% close-encounter reduction) are all AR1-vs-AR1-ablation comparisons.

Can we just plug this in?

  ⎿  Read src/alpamayo_r1/test_inference.py (78 lines)
  ⎿  Referenced file src/alpamayo_r1/models/alpamayo_r1.py
  ⎿  Referenced file src/alpamayo_r1/models/base_model.py

  ⎿  Read src/alpamayo_r1/models/action_in_proj.py (170 lines)

⏺ Short answer: no — you have most of the training scaffolding, but four specific pieces are missing.

  What the repo gives you
  - Full SFT pipeline (stage 1 VLM + discrete traj tokens/CoC, stage 2 frozen-VLM flow-matching expert): finetune/sft/
  - Full RL pipeline (GRPO via Cosmos-RL with ADE + comfort rewards): finetune/rl/
  - Trajectory tokenizers (DeltaTrajectoryTokenizer, DiscreteTrajectoryTokenizer), action space, action expert, flow-matching — all backbone-agnostic
  - The 10B teacher (nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B) for generating labels/trajectories
  - PAI dataset download + loader for images + egomotion

  What's missing for the 0.5B variant

  1. Backbone is hardcoded to Qwen3-VL. base_model.py::_initialize_qwenvl3_vlm constructs Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration directly. To use Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B you'd need a parallel init path
  (Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration / Qwen2_5_VLConfig) and to update position-id handling — sft_alpamayo_r1.py already has a _process_position_ids_qwen2_5_vl helper, so some plumbing
  was anticipated but not wired through.
  2. Chain-of-Causation text labels are not in the released data loader. PAIDataset and PhysicalAIAVDatasetLocalInterface only read egomotion + cameras; other feature readers log "not
  implemented yet." The RL README says reasoning labels will be released later. Stage 1 SFT needs CoC text to train; without it you'd either (a) skip CoC, or (b) generate traces from the
  10B teacher yourself.
  3. No distillation code exists. No teacher/student wrappers, no KD/KL loss, no logit-matching — grep for distill|teacher|student|kd_loss|kl_div returns nothing. You'd build this
  yourself (SFT on 10B-generated CoC + traj tokens is the simplest path; true logit distillation is more work because the action expert is a separate decoder).
  4. No public Cosmos-Reason-0.5B. The paper's 0.5B run presumably started from a physical-AI-pretrained Qwen2.5-VL-0.5B that NVIDIA didn't release. You'd start from vanilla
  Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct (no 0.5B official VL checkpoint exists publicly either — Qwen2.5-VL ships at 3B/7B/32B/72B), which is a real gap vs. the paper's setup.

  Practical path if you want to try anyway: swap in Qwen2.5-VL-3B, use the 10B teacher to generate CoC + trajectory tokens on PAI clips, run the existing two-stage SFT, then optionally
  RL. You will not reproduce the paper's 0.5B result exactly — different base, no physical-AI pretrain, self-generated labels.

✻ Crunched for 2m 13s

❯ What about just running the Cosmos-Reason2-2B or 8B model that is released? How much worse would that be and what baselines do they say in the paper?                                    

⏺ Fetch(https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00088)
  ⎿  Received 52.8KB (200 OK)

⏺ Fetch(https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.00088)
  ⎿  Received 8.4MB (200 OK)

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Important clarification first: the released "Alpamayo-R1-10B" is already the Cosmos-Reason2-8B backbone (8B VLM + 2B flow-matching expert = 10B total). The repo's RL config confirms
  this with vlm_name_or_path: nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-8B. So "just running" Cosmos-Reason2-8B is exactly what you already get when you use the released weights — nothing smaller to train.

  The only released size variants in the paper are:
  - Alpamayo-R1-10B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B backbone, released)
  - Alpamayo-R1-0.5B (smaller backbone for comparison, also released on HF as a public-benchmark variant)

  Table 10 — the 0.5B vs 10B head-to-head (public PhysicalAI-AV):

  ┌────────────────────────┬───────┬───────┬────────────────────┐
  │         Metric         │ 0.5B  │  10B  │        Gap         │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤
  │ minADE₆@6.4s ↓         │ 0.913 │ 0.849 │ 7% worse at 0.5B   │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤
  │ Close-encounter rate ↓ │ 9.0%  │ 4.0%  │ 2.25× more at 0.5B │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤
  │ Off-road rate ↓        │ 19.0% │ 16.0% │ 19% worse          │
  ├────────────────────────┼───────┼───────┼────────────────────┤
  │ AlpaSim score ↑        │ 0.35  │ 0.72  │ ~½ at 0.5B         │
  └────────────────────────┴───────┴───────┴────────────────────┘

  So the closed-loop safety gap is large — open-loop minADE looks close (7%), but at-fault close encounters and overall AlpaSim score roughly double when you shrink the backbone.

  Figure 12 — general-purpose VLM scaling (no Cosmos-Reason, same training recipe):
  - DINOv2 + Qwen2.5-0.5B: minADE@6s = 0.878
  - Qwen2.5-VL-3B: 0.819 (−6.7%)
  - Qwen2.5-VL-7B: 0.777 (−11.5%)

  Cosmos-Reason pretraining adds on top (§6.5.3 / Table 11): at 7B scale, Cosmos-Reason-7B hits 66.2% on LingoQA vs Qwen2.5-VL-7B's 62.2% and Qwen2-VL-7B's 52.6% — so physical-AI
  pretraining is worth ~4 points at the same size.

  About Cosmos-Reason2-2B: NVIDIA hasn't released a 2B variant of Cosmos-Reason2 publicly (only 7B/8B on HF). So your realistic options are:
  1. Use the released 10B as-is (best, free)
  2. Train a 0.5B variant — expect roughly the paper's 0.5B numbers (0.913 minADE, 9% close-encounter rate)
  3. There's no middle tier you can reach without training Cosmos-Reason yourself

  Baselines the paper reports against are internal trajectory-only / meta-action-only variants at matched size, not external models like EMMA/DriveVLM — they cite those in related work
  but don't run head-to-head. The headline numbers (12% planning accuracy gain, 35% close-encounter reduction) are all AR1-vs-AR1-ablation comparisons.

✻ Sautéed for 1m 41s

❯ For our use case we just need <3.0 for minADE₆@6.4s. NVIDIA actually has released the 2B variant here: https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-2B. Can we just plug this in?        
                                                                                                                                                                                    
⏺ Fetch(https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-2B)                                                                                                                                   
  ⎿  Received 239.7KB (200 OK)                                                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Architecture-wise: yes, it's a drop-in swap. Cosmos-Reason2-2B is Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct post-trained by NVIDIA — same Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration class that
  base_model.py::_initialize_qwenvl3_vlm already instantiates. You'd change one field:                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  # finetune/rl/hydra_configs/alpamayo1_rvla_rl_pai.yaml (and sft configs)                                                                                                                 
  vlm_name_or_path: nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-2B                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  But "plug in" ≠ "use the released weights." The released nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B checkpoint is trained against the 8B backbone's hidden dim. You cannot load those weights onto a 2B      
  backbone — you'd need to retrain from scratch (stage 1 SFT + stage 2 expert SFT, optionally RL).                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Things that will need to track the smaller backbone:                                                                                                                                     
  1. Action-expert hidden size. The paper says the expert uses "the same number of attention heads and attention dimensions, but with a smaller embedding size." Its in-proj still has to
  match the VLM's hidden dim for the KV-cache conditioning. Check finetune/sft/configs/models/ar1_base.yaml and update expert dims if they were pinned to 8B.                              
  2. Vocab extension. Stage 1 extends the text vocab by traj_vocab_size=4000 — fine, but it re-initializes new embeddings, so starting from 2B just means a smaller base before the
  extension.                                                                                                                                                                               
  3. Tokenizer. Qwen3-VL-2B and 8B share the Qwen3 tokenizer, so no changes there.                                                                                                         
  4. DeepSpeed/FSDP configs. 2B fits easily; you can probably drop ZeRO-2 or shrink batch divisors.
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Your <3.0 bar is trivially met. The paper's 0.5B variant already hits 0.913 minADE₆@6.4s, the 10B hits 0.849, and Fig 12's weakest backbone (DINOv2 + Qwen2.5-0.5B) is 0.878 at 6s. A    
  Cosmos-Reason2-2B-based AR1 will comfortably land in the 0.85–0.91 range on open-loop minADE — well under 3.0.                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  The real blocker is still what we discussed earlier: the released PAI data loader doesn't expose the CoC reasoning text labels (the RL README says "reasoning labels coming soon").      
  Without those you either skip CoC (stage 1 becomes traj-tokens-only, losing the reasoning gains) or generate CoC traces by running the released 10B teacher over clips first. If minADE
  is all you care about and you don't need the reasoning traces, skipping CoC is fine — the trajectory-only baseline in Table 6 is 0.971m at 0.5B, still far below 3.0.                    
                                                                  
✻ Baked for 52s                   

❯ Hey I don't want to do new training because it is expensive I just want to deploy something that I can run fast on a Jetson Thor Dev Kit. What are my options here?                      
                                                                                                                                                                      
⏺ Deployment options, no retraining:                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                  
  1. Deploy released nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-10B as-is                                                                                                                                          
  - bf16 weights are ~20GB; Thor's 128GB unified memory handles it easily.
  - Paper measures 99ms on RTX 6000 Pro Blackwell (Table 14). Thor Blackwell has roughly half the FP8/FP4 throughput of RTX 6000 Pro, so expect ~200–350ms end-to-end in bf16. Likely out  
  of the 100ms real-time window but runnable.                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  2. FP8/FP4 quantize with TensorRT-LLM (the actual Thor path)                                                                                                                             
  - Thor is built for FP4/FP8; TensorRT-LLM has first-class Qwen3-VL support. Convert the VLM half of the model, keep the action expert in bf16 (it's tiny — 2B, 8.75ms already).          
  - Realistic target: ~80–150ms end-to-end, no accuracy regression worth worrying about for your <3.0m bar.                                                                                
  - This is the path NVIDIA's own deployment story in §6.8 ("on-vehicle road tests") implicitly assumes.                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  3. Skip CoC at inference for a big latency cut                                                                                                                                           
  - Table 14 breakdown on RTX 6000 Pro: Reasoning decoding = 70ms of the 99ms total. Vision + prefill + trajectory flow-matching alone = 29ms.                                             
  - You can short-circuit sample_trajectories_from_data_with_vlm_rollout to not generate the CoC text trace — emit <|cot_end|> immediately after prefill, then run the expert. The         
  trajectory-only path still produces kinematically feasible outputs from the VLM's KV cache.                                                                                              
  - Cost: you lose the ~12% planning-accuracy and 35% close-encounter gains that come from explicit reasoning (Table 7/8). For a <3.0m minADE bar it's irrelevant — even the CoC-less      
  baseline is 0.971m at 0.5B.                                                                                                                                                        
  - On Thor, this gets you into the ~30–60ms zone with FP8.                                                                                                                                
                                                                  
  4. Alpamayo-R1-0.5B                                                                                                                                                                      
  - The paper's Table 10 evaluates it as "publicly released," but last I checked only the 10B is on HF. Worth confirming — if NVIDIA did push the 0.5B checkpoint, that's the obvious      
  Thor-friendly option (0.913 minADE, would run well under 50ms on Thor). Check huggingface.co/nvidia/Alpamayo-R1-0.5B directly.                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  My recommendation for Thor Dev Kit, given your <3.0m bar:                                                                                                                                
  Start with (2) — FP8 quantize the released 10B via TensorRT-LLM, keep CoC on for safety-critical behavior. If latency is still tight, add (3) as a toggle: run full CoC in               
  reasoning-critical scenarios (complex intersections), skip it on highway cruise. Thor's memory means you don't need to compromise on model size.                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                           
✻ Brewed for 46s                                                                                                                                                                           
                                                                  
❯ Last time I tried running the 10B model I got 1 Hz on the Thor and 1.5Hz if I skipped CoC inference. How do I do #2 and will this get me down to 10 Hz, maybe I had a mistake?           
                                                                                                                                                                                
⏺ 1 Hz suggests the bottleneck isn't where you think. On paper-optimized RTX 6000 Pro Blackwell, skipping CoC takes you from 99ms → 29ms (3.4× speedup). You're seeing 1000ms → 670ms      
  (1.5×). That means CoC decode is not your dominant cost right now — something upstream is (vision encoder, prefill, eager-mode attention, dtype, cache handling, or data movement). Fix
  that before worrying about TRT-LLM.                                                                                                                                                      
                              
  Profile first (half an hour's work, highest-leverage step):                                                                                                                              
  torch.cuda.synchronize(); t0 = time.perf_counter()                                                                                                                                       
  # vision encoder (visual tokens)                                                                                                                                                         
  torch.cuda.synchronize(); t1 = time.perf_counter()                                                                                                                                       
  # VLM prefill (no generation)                                                                                                                                                            
  torch.cuda.synchronize(); t2 = time.perf_counter()
  # CoC decode N tokens                                                                                                                                                                    
  torch.cuda.synchronize(); t3 = time.perf_counter()              
  # expert flow-matching (5 steps)                                                                                                                                                         
  torch.cuda.synchronize(); t4 = time.perf_counter()                                                                                                                                       
  If vision or prefill is >300ms you've found it. Common culprits on Thor: eager SDPA instead of FlashAttention, images not pre-resized to the Cosmos-Reason input res, bf16 autocast on
  layers that silently fall back to fp32.                                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  How to do FP8 via TensorRT-LLM on Thor (the real #2):                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  1. Install TRT-LLM for Thor — it has native Qwen3-VL support. trtllm-build with --use_fp8_context_fmha enable --gemm_plugin fp8. Thor has hardware FP8 tensor cores; bf16 leaves ~2× on  
  the table.                                                                                                                                                                               
  2. Quantize Qwen3-VL-8B to FP8 using ModelOpt's PTQ (quantize.py --qformat fp8). ~5 min on a few hundred calibration samples. Build a TRT engine from the quantized checkpoint.          
  3. Leave the action expert in PyTorch bf16. It's 2B, only 5 flow-matching steps, ~9ms on RTX 6000 Pro — not worth the integration cost, and the KV-cache handoff between TRT and PyTorch 
  is simpler if you only TRT the VLM.                                                                                                                                                      
  4. KV-cache handoff is the tricky part. The code calls self.vlm.generate() then crops the cache at <|traj_future_start|> and feeds it to the expert. With TRT-LLM you swap that for      
  ModelRunner.generate() and extract KV from its paged cache. tensorrt_llm.runtime.ModelRunner exposes it via get_kv_cache. Some rewiring in sample_trajectories_from_data_with_vlm_rollout
   required.                                                      
  5. Turn on speculative decoding or reduce CoC budget. You have max_generation_length=256 in the test script; actual CoC traces are ~40 tokens. Cap it at 48–64 and you halve decode time 
  immediately, before any other optimization.                                                                                                                                              
   
  Realistic Thor latency budget in FP8 TRT-LLM:                                                                                                                                            
  - Vision (fused ViT): ~8–12ms                                   
  - Prefill (a few hundred tokens, FP8): ~25–40ms                                                                                                                                          
  - CoC decode 40 tokens FP8: ~60–100ms (memory-bandwidth-bound; Thor has ~273 GB/s vs RTX 6000 Pro's ~1.8 TB/s — this is the one thing FP8 helps a lot with)
  - Expert flow-matching: ~15–20ms                                                                                                                                                         
  - Total with CoC: 110–170ms → 6–9 Hz. 10 Hz with full CoC on Thor is genuinely hard.                                                                                                     
  - Total without CoC: 50–75ms → 13–20 Hz. This is where you comfortably clear 10 Hz.                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  If 10 Hz with CoC is a hard requirement, the realistic lever is shortening the trace, not quantizing harder. At 15–20 CoC tokens you'd be at ~80–110ms with FP8, which crosses 10 Hz. The
   paper doesn't study how much CoC can be truncated before quality degrades, so you'd have to A/B it on your ADE target.                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  One last thing worth checking on your 1 Hz result: is attn_implementation="flash_attention_2" set when loading the model? The from_pretrained in test_inference.py doesn't pass it — HF  
  defaults to eager SDPA, which on Thor is multiple times slower than FA2 for long sequences. That alone could explain most of your gap.
                                                                                                                                                                                           
✻ Churned for 52s                                                 

❯ Alright great well to test this I want you to deploy a script to my Modal account, download about 30s of video from the Physical AV dataset and run live inference on that 30s of video,

⏺ Before I spend time and your Modal credits on this, I need to flag something and check two things.                                                                                       
                                               
  Flag: Modal ≠ Thor. Modal runs H100/A100/L40S — roughly 5–10× faster than Jetson Thor for this workload. Hitting 6+ Hz on Modal tells you almost nothing about whether you'll hit 6+ Hz  
  on Thor. If the goal is "validate the Thor latency target," Modal is the wrong benchmark target. Three possibilities:
  1. You just want to validate the pipeline works end-to-end and measure an upper bound — Modal is fine.                                                                                   
  2. You want to iterate on the CoC-skip / FA2 / FP8 optimizations without burning Thor dev time — Modal is OK as a dev environment, but absolute Hz numbers won't transfer; only relative 
  speedups from optimizations will be roughly comparable.                                                                                                                                  
  3. You want a proxy for Thor — this won't work; pick a closer target (e.g., Orin AGX on Modal isn't available; you'd need to benchmark on actual Thor).

That changes what I build.

  max_generation_length=256 vs 48 is basically the same wall-clock — the model stops naturally at ~40 tokens. Real CoC-skipping needs a prompt template modification (ending at            
  <|cot_end|><|traj_future_start|> directly), not a max_new_tokens cap. ⏺ Monitor event: "Modal benchmark run — watch for build/download progress, Hz result, or errors"

⏺ Image building. While I wait, one insight from reading the rest of the inference function: StopAfterEOS already halts CoC decode the moment <|traj_future_start|> is emitted, so
  max_generation_length=256 vs 48 is basically the same wall-clock — the model stops naturally at ~40 tokens. Real CoC-skipping needs a prompt template modification (ending at
  <|cot_end|><|traj_future_start|> directly), not a max_new_tokens cap. I'll add that as a proper variant after we get baseline.

⏺ I have a hypothesis. The bug: in v4.57.1, _is_packed_sequence returns True for Qwen3-VL because mrope T-positions have repeats across image tokens — but these aren't packed sequences,  
  they're just a single sequence with mixed modalities. The varlen path then mis-constructs cu_seqlens.                                                                                    
                                            
  Fix: monkey-patch _is_packed_sequence to return False for single-batch inference. Non-varlen FA2 is correct for bs=1.                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Update(scripts/modal_benchmark.py)                                                                                                                                                       
  ⎿  Added 15 lines                                                                                                                                                                        
       99      import torch                                                                                                                                                                
      100      import numpy as np                                                                                                                                                          
      101                                                                                                                                                                                  
      102 +    # Monkey-patch transformers FA2 varlen detection for Qwen3-VL.                                                                                                              
      103 +    # In 4.57.1, `_is_packed_sequence(text_position_ids)` returns True for bs=1 because                                                                                  
      104 +    # mrope T-positions repeat across image tokens. This triggers the varlen FA2 path                                                                                           
      105 +    # which then feeds a malformed cu_seqlens to flash_attn → "cu_seqlens_q must have                                                                                    
      106 +    # shape (batch_size + 1)". For bs=1 inference this is spurious — the sequence is a                                                                                          
      107 +    # single (mixed-modality) stream, not packed examples. Disable the detection for                                                                                            
      108 +    # bs=1 so FA2 uses the non-varlen fast path.                                                                                                                                
      109 +    import transformers.modeling_flash_attention_utils as _fa_utils                                                                                                             
      110 +    _orig_is_packed = _fa_utils._is_packed_sequence                                                                                                                             
      111 +    def _is_packed_sequence_patched(position_ids, batch_size):                                                                                                           
      112 +        if batch_size == 1:                                                                                                                                              
      113 +            return False                                                                                                                                                 
      114 +        return _orig_is_packed(position_ids, batch_size)                                                                                                                 
      115 +    _fa_utils._is_packed_sequence = _is_packed_sequence_patched                                                                                                          
      116 +

⏺ Update(scripts/modal_benchmark.py)                                                                                                                                                       
  ⎿  Added 28 lines                                                                      
      191          raise RuntimeError("no samples preloaded")                      
      192      print(f"[bench] preloaded {len(preloaded)} samples")
      193                                     
      194 +    # Per-stage timing via monkey-patch. `stage_times` accumulates ms for the                                                                                            
      195 +    # last run_one call; we reset it each iteration.                                                                                                                     
      196 +    stage_times = {"vlm_ms": 0.0, "diff_ms": 0.0}                                                                                                                               
      197 +    _orig_vlm_generate = model.vlm.generate                                                                                                                              
      198 +    _orig_diff_sample = model.diffusion.sample                                                                                                                                  
      199 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      200 +    def _timed_vlm_generate(*a, **kw):                                                                                                                                          
      201 +        torch.cuda.synchronize()                                                                                                                                         
      202 +        t = time.perf_counter()                                                                                                                                                 
      203 +        out = _orig_vlm_generate(*a, **kw)                                                                                                                               
      204 +        torch.cuda.synchronize()                                                                                                                                         
      205 +        stage_times["vlm_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - t) * 1000                                                                                                         
      206 +        return out                                                                                                                                                       
      207 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      208 +    def _timed_diff_sample(*a, **kw):                                                                                                                                    
      209 +        torch.cuda.synchronize()                                                                                                                                         
      210 +        t = time.perf_counter()                                                                                                                                          
      211 +        out = _orig_diff_sample(*a, **kw)                                                                                                                                
      212 +        torch.cuda.synchronize()                                                                                                                                         
      213 +        stage_times["diff_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - t) * 1000                                                                                                        
      214 +        return out                                                                                                                                                       
      215 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      216 +    model.vlm.generate = _timed_vlm_generate                                                                                                                             
      217 +    model.diffusion.sample = _timed_diff_sample                                                                                                                          
      218 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      219 +    diffusion_kwargs = {"inference_step": diffusion_steps} if diffusion_steps > 0 else None                                                                              
      220 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      221      def run_one(sample):
      222          # sample's tokenized_data gets mutated by sample_traj (input_ids popped),
      223          # so we pass a shallow copy to keep the preloaded dict reusable.
     ...
      239                  num_traj_samples=num_traj_samples,
      240                  max_generation_length=max_gen,
      241                  return_extra=True,
      242 +                diffusion_kwargs=diffusion_kwargs,                                                                                                                       
      243              )
      244          return pred_xyz
      245

⏺ Linear scaling confirmed. Diffusion went 714ms → 262ms (2.7x) at 4 vs 10 steps. Final picture:
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  ┌───────────────────────────┬──────┬─────┬──────┬─────────────┐                             
  │          Config           │  Hz  │ VLM │ Diff │    Notes    │                                                                                                                          
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────┼─────┼──────┼─────────────┤                             
  │ Full CoC (256), 10 diff   │ 0.60 │ —   │ —    │ Baseline    │                                                                                                                          
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────┼─────┼──────┼─────────────┤                             
  │ Skip CoC, 10 diff         │ 1.16 │ 436 │ 714  │ No CoC cost │                                                                                                                          
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────┼─────┼──────┼─────────────┤                             
  │ Skip CoC, 4 diff          │ 1.42 │ 435 │ 262  │ Best so far │                                                                                                                          
  ├───────────────────────────┼──────┼─────┼──────┼─────────────┤                             
  │ Skip CoC, 1 diff (extrap) │ ~2.3 │ 435 │ ~65  │ Floor       │                                                                                                                          
  └───────────────────────────┴──────┴─────┴──────┴─────────────┘                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Key finding: VLM prefill is the hard floor at ~435ms. With 0 diffusion steps, upper bound on A100-40GB is ~2.3 Hz. 6Hz (167ms budget) is not reachable at bf16 without attacking the VLM 
  prefill.                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  The VLM cost is dominated by ~12k image tokens (4 cameras × 3 frames). Paths to 6Hz:                                                                                                     
  1. FP8/INT8 quantize VLM — typical 1.5–2x → 3–4 Hz (still short)                                                                                                                         
  2. Fewer/smaller camera frames — linear in token count, but changes model input distribution                                                                                             
  3. H100 — ~1.5–2x over A100 → 2.1–3 Hz on current code                                      
  4. KV cache reuse across frames — frames share overlap; would need architectural work                                                                                                    
                                                                                         
  Your 10Hz target on Jetson Thor at bf16 with full model is almost certainly infeasible. Realistic path: FP8 quantization + aggressive vision-token pruning + TRT-LLM graph on Thor.      
                                                                                         
  Want me to try quantization next (FP8 via transformers.BitsAndBytes or native on A100), or wrap it here and write up a recommendation doc?                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Monitor "Alpamayo skip-CoC + diffusion_steps=4" stream ended
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Monitor ended cleanly. Awaiting your call on next step.                                
                                                                                                                                                                                           
※ recap: Goal: maximize Alpamayo-R1-10B inference Hz on A100 toward 6+ Hz. Current best is 1.42 Hz (skip-CoC, 4 diffusion steps); VLM prefill caps ceiling near 2.3 Hz. Next: decide       
  whether to try FP8 quantization or stop and write up.                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                           
❯ We need to get to 6+ Hz.   ├──────┼────────┼────────────┼──────┼────────┼──────┼──────┼───────┤                                                                                                                     
  │ 1    │ 1      │ 1          │ 7.13 │ 26     │ 64   │ 46   │ 140   │                                                                                                                     
  └──────┴────────┴────────────┴──────┴────────┴──────┴──────┴───────┘                   
                                                   
  Key findings:                                                                                                                                                                            
  - Hardware can do 6+ Hz — confirmed. Floor on A100 is ~140ms.                                                                                                                            
  - VLM text prefill has a ~65ms floor (minimum decoder forward over any prompt).                                                                                                          
  - Per diffusion step ≈ 45ms; each step is linear.                                                                                                                                        
  - Vision tower is cheap (~25-45ms) — NOT the bottleneck.                               
  - Dominant costs are decoder prefill (scales with input tokens) and diffusion step count.                                                                                                
                                                                                           
  Open question — accuracy: We don't know if 1 cam × 1 frame × 1 diff step produces usable trajectories. Model was trained on 4 cams × 4 frames × 10 diff. Need minADE evaluation at each  
  config to find the accuracy/speed sweet spot.                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Recommendation for 6-10 Hz production:                                                 
  1. Run minADE sweep across these configs to find minimum viable inputs.                                                                                                                  
  2. Expect sweet spot around 2 cams × 2 frames × 2 diff steps → ~4 Hz at bf16.
  3. To push that to 6+ Hz: add FP8 quantization (typically 1.5-2x on A100, ~2x on H100) → 6-8 Hz with reasonable inputs.                                                                  
  4. On Thor: same input reduction + TRT-LLM + FP8 is the realistic path to 10 Hz.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Want me to (a) run a minADE sweep next, (b) wrap up with a written recommendation, or (c) try FP8 quantization?                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Monitor "Alpamayo floor: 1 cam + 1 frame + 1 diff step" stream ended
