   on Thor; the light config could hit 3–5 Hz.                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                           
❯ great from now on we don't want to do CoC. What other ways could we make this run faster on the H100? It seems like to have decent minADE, we are going to need to run 4 cams, 4 frames, 
   and 4 diffusion steps. Make a table with expected speedup and quality degradation for each option. (give me the top 10 options)                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Baseline to beat: 4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC on H100 ≈ ~310 ms gpu_inf (3.2 Hz). Dropping 10→4 diff steps already gives ~1.25× vs the 405 ms number you saw.                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Rough decomposition of that 310 ms (estimated, not profiled yet): vision ~160 ms, VLM prefill ~90 ms, expert diffusion @ 4 steps ~60 ms.                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  ┌─────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┬────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐      
  │  #  │                  Optimization                  │    Expected speedup    │   Expected ADE impact    │ Effort │     Retrain?     │                  Notes                   │      
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │ torch.compile VLM + expert                     │                        │                          │        │                  │ First-call compile cost ~30s. Needs      │  
  │ 1   │ (mode="reduce-overhead", dynamic=False, static │ 1.20–1.40×             │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ static shapes — you already use fixed    │      
  │     │  image grid)                                   │                        │                          │        │                  │ 4cam/4fr.                                │      
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │                                                │ 1.20–1.40× on          │                          │        │                  │ transformers 4.57 supports fa3 on H100.  │      
  │ 2   │ FlashAttention 3 for VLM                       │ attn-heavy stages →    │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ Expert still needs SDPA (custom 4D       │      
  │     │                                                │ 1.10–1.20× total       │                          │        │                  │ mask).                                   │  
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │ FP8 (TransformerEngine) on VLM                 │ 1.40–1.70× on VLM      │ 2–8% (well-calibrated),  │        │                  │ H100's native format. The ViT + 8B text  │      
  │ 3   │ weights+activations                            │ prefill + ViT →        │ up to 15% naive          │ Med    │ calibration only │ decoder are the biggest win.             │      
  │     │                                                │ 1.25–1.45× total       │                          │        │                  │                                          │      
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ 4   │ FP8 on expert only                             │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ ~2–5%                    │ Med    │ calibration      │ Expert is smaller, smaller win, but very │  
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │  safe.                                   │  
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │                                                │ 1.40–1.80× on VLM →    │                          │        │                  │ Real win comes from fused kernels +      │  
  │ 5   │ TensorRT-LLM export of VLM (ViT + text)        │ 1.25–1.50× total       │ none if fully exported   │ High   │ no               │ graph. Action expert doesn't export      │      
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ cleanly — keep in PyTorch.               │      
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤  
  │     │ Reduce Qwen3-VL pixel budget                   │ 1.40–1.80× on vision → │ 5–20% (depends on        │        │ no (but RL/SFT   │ Fewer image tokens cuts ViT and shrinks  │      
  │ 6   │ (min_pixels/max_pixels, e.g. 448² → 336²)      │  1.20–1.40× total      │ distant-obstacle detail) │ Low    │ would help       │ VLM prefill.                             │      
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │ regain)          │                                          │  
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ Eliminates per-forward kernel launch     │  
  │ 7   │ CUDA graphs + static KV cache for VLM          │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ none                     │ Med    │ no               │ overhead. Works well once shapes are     │      
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ pinned.                                  │  
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ 8   │ Distill expert to fewer diffusion steps        │ 1.05–1.10× total       │ 3–10%                    │ High   │ yes              │ Diffusion is only ~20% of total at 4     │      
  │     │ (consistency/LCM, 4→2)                         │                        │                          │        │                  │ steps — smaller leverage than it sounds. │  
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │     │ Vision-token reuse across adjacent ticks       │ 1.50–2.50× on vision → │ 3–10% (mostly on         │        │                  │ Practical on highway/straight driving;   │ 
  │ 9   │ (reuse t-1's ViT output for 3 of 4 cams when   │  1.30–1.60× total in   │ fast-changing scenes)    │ Med    │ no               │ disable during turns/intersections.      │ 
  │     │ egomotion < threshold)                         │ steady driving         │                          │        │                  │                                          │      
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤ 
  │ 10  │ Distill VLM backbone to 3B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B  │ 2.0–2.5× total         │ 5–15%                    │ Very   │ yes              │ Biggest ceiling lift. Pays for itself on │      
  │     │ → Qwen3-VL-3B or similar)                      │                        │                          │ High   │                  │  Thor where the VLM dominates.           │      
  └─────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Stacking guidance (no retrain): 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 together are realistic to combine and should land ~1.8–2.3× total → ~140–170 ms gpu_inf → 6–7 Hz on H100 with ADE essentially unchanged.
  Add 6 (reduced pixels) if you can tolerate modest quality loss → push past 8 Hz.                                                                                                         
                                                 
  Caveat: these ranges are priors based on how Qwen3-VL-scale models typically respond to each intervention, not measurements on this model. The right next step is to actually profile the
   4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC H100 run with a stage breakdown (we have vlm_ms / vision_ms / diff_ms timers already in benchmark()) so the ranking reflects real bottlenecks instead of          
  estimates.

  which optimizations to actually implement.

⏺ Let me add a prefix resolver and wire it into the videos entrypoint so you can pass 25cd4769:

⏺ Update(scripts/modal_benchmark.py)
  ⎿  Added 30 lines
      659  
      660  
      661  @app.function(
      662 +    volumes={"/cache/hf": hf_cache, "/cache/pai": pai_cache},                                                                                                            
      663 +    secrets=[modal.Secret.from_name("huggingface")],                                                                                                                     
      664 +    timeout=60 * 10,                                                                                                                                                     
      665 +)                                                                                                                                                                        
      666 +def resolve_clip_id(prefix: str) -> str:                                                                                                                                 
      667 +    """Given a clip-id prefix (e.g. '25cd4769'), return the full UUID. Raises if                                                                                         
      668 +    no match or ambiguous match."""                                                                                                                                      
      669 +    import os                                                                                                                                                            
      670 +    os.environ["HF_HOME"] = "/cache/hf"                                                                                                                                  
      671 +    os.environ["HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE"] = "/cache/hf/hub"                                                                                                                
      672 +    os.environ["PAI_CACHE"] = "/cache/pai"                                                                                                                               
      673 +    if "HF_TOKEN" not in os.environ and "HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN" in os.environ:                                                                                          
      674 +        os.environ["HF_TOKEN"] = os.environ["HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN"]                                                                                                    
      675 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      676 +    import physical_ai_av                                                                                                                                                
      677 +    avdi = physical_ai_av.PhysicalAIAVDatasetInterface()                                                                                                                 
      678 +    ci = getattr(avdi, "clip_index", None)                                                                                                                               
      679 +    if ci is None:                                                                                                                                                       
      680 +        raise RuntimeError("avdi has no clip_index")                                                                                                                     
      681 +    all_ids = ci.index.tolist() if hasattr(ci, "index") else list(ci)                                                                                                    
      682 +    matches = [c for c in all_ids if str(c).startswith(prefix)]                                                                                                          
      683 +    if not matches:                                                                                                                                                      
      684 +        raise RuntimeError(f"no clip matches prefix {prefix!r}")                                                                                                         
      685 +    if len(matches) > 1:                                                                                                                                                 
      686 +        raise RuntimeError(f"prefix {prefix!r} matches {len(matches)} clips: {matches[:5]}")                                                                             
      687 +    print(f"[resolve] {prefix!r} -> {matches[0]}")                                                                                                                       
      688 +    return matches[0]                                                                                                                                                    
      689 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      690 +                                                                                                                                                                         
      691 +@app.function(                                                                                                                                                           
      692      gpu="A100-40GB",
      693      volumes={"/cache/hf": hf_cache, "/cache/pai": pai_cache, "/outputs": videos_vol},
      694      secrets=[modal.Secret.from_name("huggingface")],

⏺ Update(scripts/modal_benchmark.py)
  ⎿  Added 5 lines, removed 1 line
      1131  ):
      1132      """Precache frames to the PAI volume (idempotent), then render + save one
      1133      real-time simulation MP4 per config. Defaults cover the full usable window
      1134 -    of one ~20s PAI clip: 1.7s (history safety margin) to 19.7s."""                                                                                                     
      1134 +    of one ~20s PAI clip: 1.7s (history safety margin) to 19.7s. `clip_id` may                                                                                          
      1135 +    be a UUID prefix — it's resolved to the full UUID via the PAI clip index."""                                                                                        
      1136 +    if len(clip_id) < 36:                                                                                                                                               
      1137 +        clip_id = resolve_clip_id.remote(clip_id)                                                                                                                       
      1138 +        print(f"[local] resolved clip_id: {clip_id}")                                                                                                                   
      1139      if not skip_precache:
      1140          print(f"[local] precaching clip={clip_id} [{t0_start_s:.2f}s, "
      1141                f"{t0_start_s+duration_s:.2f}s] ...")

⏺ Ablation done. Side-by-side (clip 25cd4769, 18s window, A100-40GB):

  config              CoC?  | gpu_inf  car_gpu_hz  sim_hz  ADE(m)  n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff     no    | 1040 ms      0.96     1.00    1.52     18
  4cam_4fr_10diff     yes   | 1557 ms      0.64     0.67    2.01     12
  1cam_1fr_1diff      no    |  192 ms      5.20     5.22    4.56     94
  1cam_1fr_1diff      yes   |  792 ms      1.26     1.28    4.44     23

  Takeaways:
  - CoC adds ~500–600ms of autoregressive VLM decoding per pred. Proportional hit is brutal on the small config (5.2 Hz → 1.3 Hz, −75%), modest on the heavy one (1.0 Hz → 0.67 Hz, −33%).
  - ADE didn't improve with CoC on this clip (4cam went 1.52→2.01, 1cam 4.56→4.44). Sample counts are small (12–94) so single-clip deltas are noisy — not a general claim about CoC
  quality.
  - Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769/ (no-CoC) and alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_withcot/ (CoC).

⏺ H100 no-CoC done. Side-by-side vs A100-40GB no-CoC:

  config           GPU          | gpu_inf  car_gpu_hz  sim_hz   ADE(m)  n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff  A100-40GB    | 1040 ms      0.96    1.00     1.52      18
  4cam_4fr_10diff  H100         |  405 ms      2.47    2.50     1.56      45
  1cam_1fr_1diff   A100-40GB    |  192 ms      5.20    5.22     4.56      94
  1cam_1fr_1diff   H100         |   85 ms     11.72   11.72     4.62     211

  H100 speedup: 2.57× heavy / 2.25× light. Quality essentially identical (ADE within noise). Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_h100/.

  Thor reality check: H100 is ~3–4× Thor's raw compute, so the H100 numbers roughly bracket what well-tuned (TRT + FP8) Thor inference might reach. Heavy config likely still sub-real-time
   on Thor; the light config could hit 3–5 Hz.

❯ great from now on we don't want to do CoC. What other ways could we make this run faster on the H100? It seems like to have decent minADE, we are going to need to run 4 cams, 4 frames, 
   and 4 diffusion steps. Make a table with expected speedup and quality degradation for each option. (give me the top 10 options)                                                         

⏺ Baseline to beat: 4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC on H100 ≈ ~310 ms gpu_inf (3.2 Hz). Dropping 10→4 diff steps already gives ~1.25× vs the 405 ms number you saw.

  Rough decomposition of that 310 ms (estimated, not profiled yet): vision ~160 ms, VLM prefill ~90 ms, expert diffusion @ 4 steps ~60 ms.

  ┌─────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┬────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │                  Optimization                  │    Expected speedup    │   Expected ADE impact    │ Effort │     Retrain?     │                  Notes                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ torch.compile VLM + expert                     │                        │                          │        │                  │ First-call compile cost ~30s. Needs      │
  │ 1   │ (mode="reduce-overhead", dynamic=False, static │ 1.20–1.40×             │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ static shapes — you already use fixed    │
  │     │  image grid)                                   │                        │                          │        │                  │ 4cam/4fr.                                │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │ 1.20–1.40× on          │                          │        │                  │ transformers 4.57 supports fa3 on H100.  │
  │ 2   │ FlashAttention 3 for VLM                       │ attn-heavy stages →    │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ Expert still needs SDPA (custom 4D       │
  │     │                                                │ 1.10–1.20× total       │                          │        │                  │ mask).                                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ FP8 (TransformerEngine) on VLM                 │ 1.40–1.70× on VLM      │ 2–8% (well-calibrated),  │        │                  │ H100's native format. The ViT + 8B text  │
  │ 3   │ weights+activations                            │ prefill + ViT →        │ up to 15% naive          │ Med    │ calibration only │ decoder are the biggest win.             │
  │     │                                                │ 1.25–1.45× total       │                          │        │                  │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 4   │ FP8 on expert only                             │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ ~2–5%                    │ Med    │ calibration      │ Expert is smaller, smaller win, but very │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │  safe.                                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │ 1.40–1.80× on VLM →    │                          │        │                  │ Real win comes from fused kernels +      │
  │ 5   │ TensorRT-LLM export of VLM (ViT + text)        │ 1.25–1.50× total       │ none if fully exported   │ High   │ no               │ graph. Action expert doesn't export      │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ cleanly — keep in PyTorch.               │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Reduce Qwen3-VL pixel budget                   │ 1.40–1.80× on vision → │ 5–20% (depends on        │        │ no (but RL/SFT   │ Fewer image tokens cuts ViT and shrinks  │
  │ 6   │ (min_pixels/max_pixels, e.g. 448² → 336²)      │  1.20–1.40× total      │ distant-obstacle detail) │ Low    │ would help       │ VLM prefill.                             │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │ regain)          │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ Eliminates per-forward kernel launch     │
  │ 7   │ CUDA graphs + static KV cache for VLM          │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ none                     │ Med    │ no               │ overhead. Works well once shapes are     │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ pinned.                                  │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 8   │ Distill expert to fewer diffusion steps        │ 1.05–1.10× total       │ 3–10%                    │ High   │ yes              │ Diffusion is only ~20% of total at 4     │
  │     │ (consistency/LCM, 4→2)                         │                        │                          │        │                  │ steps — smaller leverage than it sounds. │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Vision-token reuse across adjacent ticks       │ 1.50–2.50× on vision → │ 3–10% (mostly on         │        │                  │ Practical on highway/straight driving;   │
  │ 9   │ (reuse t-1's ViT output for 3 of 4 cams when   │  1.30–1.60× total in   │ fast-changing scenes)    │ Med    │ no               │ disable during turns/intersections.      │
  │     │ egomotion < threshold)                         │ steady driving         │                          │        │                  │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 10  │ Distill VLM backbone to 3B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B  │ 2.0–2.5× total         │ 5–15%                    │ Very   │ yes              │ Biggest ceiling lift. Pays for itself on │
  │     │ → Qwen3-VL-3B or similar)                      │                        │                          │ High   │                  │  Thor where the VLM dominates.           │
  └─────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Stacking guidance (no retrain): 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 together are realistic to combine and should land ~1.8–2.3× total → ~140–170 ms gpu_inf → 6–7 Hz on H100 with ADE essentially unchanged.
  Add 6 (reduced pixels) if you can tolerate modest quality loss → push past 8 Hz.

  Caveat: these ranges are priors based on how Qwen3-VL-scale models typically respond to each intervention, not measurements on this model. The right next step is to actually profile the
   4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC H100 run with a stage breakdown (we have vlm_ms / vision_ms / diff_ms timers already in benchmark()) so the ranking reflects real bottlenecks instead of
  estimates.

✻ Cooked for 1m 27s

※ recap: Deploying Alpamayo-R1-10B for real-time inference; just benchmarked 4cam/4fr/10diff no-CoC on H100 (2.5 Hz, ADE 1.56m) and delivered a top-10 speedup options table. Next: pick
  which optimizations to actually implement.

⏺ Ablation done. Side-by-side (clip 25cd4769, 18s window, A100-40GB):

  config              CoC?  | gpu_inf  car_gpu_hz  sim_hz  ADE(m)  n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff     no    | 1040 ms      0.96     1.00    1.52     18
  4cam_4fr_10diff     yes   | 1557 ms      0.64     0.67    2.01     12
  1cam_1fr_1diff      no    |  192 ms      5.20     5.22    4.56     94
  1cam_1fr_1diff      yes   |  792 ms      1.26     1.28    4.44     23

  Takeaways:
  - CoC adds ~500–600ms of autoregressive VLM decoding per pred. Proportional hit is brutal on the small config (5.2 Hz → 1.3 Hz, −75%), modest on the heavy one (1.0 Hz → 0.67 Hz, −33%).
  - ADE didn't improve with CoC on this clip (4cam went 1.52→2.01, 1cam 4.56→4.44). Sample counts are small (12–94) so single-clip deltas are noisy — not a general claim about CoC
  quality.
  - Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769/ (no-CoC) and alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_withcot/ (CoC).

❯ okay try running the non CoC version on an h100

⏺ H100 no-CoC done. Side-by-side vs A100-40GB no-CoC:

  config           GPU          | gpu_inf  car_gpu_hz  sim_hz   ADE(m)  n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff  A100-40GB    | 1040 ms      0.96    1.00     1.52      18
  4cam_4fr_10diff  H100         |  405 ms      2.47    2.50     1.56      45
  1cam_1fr_1diff   A100-40GB    |  192 ms      5.20    5.22     4.56      94
  1cam_1fr_1diff   H100         |   85 ms     11.72   11.72     4.62     211

  H100 speedup: 2.57× heavy / 2.25× light. Quality essentially identical (ADE within noise). Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_h100/.

  Thor reality check: H100 is ~3–4× Thor's raw compute, so the H100 numbers roughly bracket what well-tuned (TRT + FP8) Thor inference might reach. Heavy config likely still sub-real-time
   on Thor; the light config could hit 3–5 Hz.

❯ great from now on we don't want to do CoC. What other ways could we make this run faster on the H100? It seems like to have decent minADE, we are going to need to run 4 cams, 4 frames, 
   and 4 diffusion steps. Make a table with expected speedup and quality degradation for each option. (give me the top 10 options)                                                         

⏺ Baseline to beat: 4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC on H100 ≈ ~310 ms gpu_inf (3.2 Hz). Dropping 10→4 diff steps already gives ~1.25× vs the 405 ms number you saw.

  Rough decomposition of that 310 ms (estimated, not profiled yet): vision ~160 ms, VLM prefill ~90 ms, expert diffusion @ 4 steps ~60 ms.

  ┌─────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┬────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │                  Optimization                  │    Expected speedup    │   Expected ADE impact    │ Effort │     Retrain?     │                  Notes                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ torch.compile VLM + expert                     │                        │                          │        │                  │ First-call compile cost ~30s. Needs      │
  │ 1   │ (mode="reduce-overhead", dynamic=False, static │ 1.20–1.40×             │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ static shapes — you already use fixed    │
  │     │  image grid)                                   │                        │                          │        │                  │ 4cam/4fr.                                │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │ 1.20–1.40× on          │                          │        │                  │ transformers 4.57 supports fa3 on H100.  │
  │ 2   │ FlashAttention 3 for VLM                       │ attn-heavy stages →    │ none                     │ Low    │ no               │ Expert still needs SDPA (custom 4D       │
  │     │                                                │ 1.10–1.20× total       │                          │        │                  │ mask).                                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ FP8 (TransformerEngine) on VLM                 │ 1.40–1.70× on VLM      │ 2–8% (well-calibrated),  │        │                  │ H100's native format. The ViT + 8B text  │
  │ 3   │ weights+activations                            │ prefill + ViT →        │ up to 15% naive          │ Med    │ calibration only │ decoder are the biggest win.             │
  │     │                                                │ 1.25–1.45× total       │                          │        │                  │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 4   │ FP8 on expert only                             │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ ~2–5%                    │ Med    │ calibration      │ Expert is smaller, smaller win, but very │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │  safe.                                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │ 1.40–1.80× on VLM →    │                          │        │                  │ Real win comes from fused kernels +      │
  │ 5   │ TensorRT-LLM export of VLM (ViT + text)        │ 1.25–1.50× total       │ none if fully exported   │ High   │ no               │ graph. Action expert doesn't export      │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ cleanly — keep in PyTorch.               │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Reduce Qwen3-VL pixel budget                   │ 1.40–1.80× on vision → │ 5–20% (depends on        │        │ no (but RL/SFT   │ Fewer image tokens cuts ViT and shrinks  │
  │ 6   │ (min_pixels/max_pixels, e.g. 448² → 336²)      │  1.20–1.40× total      │ distant-obstacle detail) │ Low    │ would help       │ VLM prefill.                             │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │ regain)          │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ Eliminates per-forward kernel launch     │
  │ 7   │ CUDA graphs + static KV cache for VLM          │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ none                     │ Med    │ no               │ overhead. Works well once shapes are     │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ pinned.                                  │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 8   │ Distill expert to fewer diffusion steps        │ 1.05–1.10× total       │ 3–10%                    │ High   │ yes              │ Diffusion is only ~20% of total at 4     │
  │     │ (consistency/LCM, 4→2)                         │                        │                          │        │                  │ steps — smaller leverage than it sounds. │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Vision-token reuse across adjacent ticks       │ 1.50–2.50× on vision → │ 3–10% (mostly on         │        │                  │ Practical on highway/straight driving;   │
  │ 9   │ (reuse t-1's ViT output for 3 of 4 cams when   │  1.30–1.60× total in   │ fast-changing scenes)    │ Med    │ no               │ disable during turns/intersections.      │
  │     │ egomotion < threshold)                         │ steady driving         │                          │        │                  │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 10  │ Distill VLM backbone to 3B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B  │ 2.0–2.5× total         │ 5–15%                    │ Very   │ yes              │ Biggest ceiling lift. Pays for itself on │
  │     │ → Qwen3-VL-3B or similar)                      │                        │                          │ High   │                  │  Thor where the VLM dominates.           │
  └─────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Stacking guidance (no retrain): 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 together are realistic to combine and should land ~1.8–2.3× total → ~140–170 ms gpu_inf → 6–7 Hz on H100 with ADE essentially unchanged.
  Add 6 (reduced pixels) if you can tolerate modest quality loss → push past 8 Hz.

  Caveat: these ranges are priors based on how Qwen3-VL-scale models typically respond to each intervention, not measurements on this model. The right next step is to actually profile the
   4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC H100 run with a stage breakdown (we have vlm_ms / vision_ms / diff_ms timers already in benchmark()) so the ranking reflects real bottlenecks instead of
  estimates.

✻ Cooked for 1m 27s

※ recap: Deploying Alpamayo-R1-10B for real-time inference; just benchmarked 4cam/4fr/10diff no-CoC on H100 (2.5 Hz, ADE 1.56m) and delivered a top-10 speedup options table. Next: pick
  which optimizations to actually implement.

Side-by-side on H100, no-CoC, clip 25cd4769:                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  config           model           | gpu_inf   Hz     ADE(m)   n_preds                                                                                                                     
  4cam_4fr_10diff  Alpamayo-R1     |  405 ms  2.50   1.56      45                                                                                                                          
  4cam_4fr_10diff  Alpamayo-1.5    |  458 ms  2.22   1.22      40                                                                                                                          
  1cam_1fr_1diff   Alpamayo-R1     |   85 ms 11.72   4.62     211                                                                                                                          
  1cam_1fr_1diff   Alpamayo-1.5    |   92 ms 10.89   7.72     196                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Takeaways:                                       
  - 4cam/4fr/10diff: Alpamayo-1.5 is ~13% slower but ~22% better ADE (1.22m vs 1.56m) — meaningful quality win.                                                                            
  - 1cam/1fr/1diff: Alpamayo-1.5 is similar speed but 67% worse ADE (7.72m vs 4.62m). Likely because 1.5's prompt embeds explicit camera-name + frame-index annotations
  (_build_image_content in helper.py) — training assumes multi-cam; feeding a single front-wide is out-of-distribution.                                                                    
  - Preprocess time jumped (335ms vs 303ms), and data_load went up too — the 1.5 processor uses min_pixels=163840 (it's the Qwen3-VL-2B processor), which causes larger image tokenization 
  per frame.                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_alp15_h100/.                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Recommendation: if you pick Alpamayo-1.5, stick to 4-cam configs — the quality advantage only materializes when the input matches its training distribution.

⏺ Looking at _build_image_content in alpamayo1_5/helper.py: it takes num_frames_per_camera (default 4) and uses it to expand camera_indices via repeat_interleave. Our build_messages never
   passes it — so for any config with n_frames ≠ 4, the prompt tells the model "you have 4 frames of this cam" when we actually passed fewer. That's likely contributing to the 1cam
  degradation.

⏺ Ablation done. Side-by-side (clip 25cd4769, 18s window, A100-40GB):

  config              CoC?  | gpu_inf  car_gpu_hz  sim_hz  ADE(m)  n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff     no    | 1040 ms      0.96     1.00    1.52     18
  4cam_4fr_10diff     yes   | 1557 ms      0.64     0.67    2.01     12
  1cam_1fr_1diff      no    |  192 ms      5.20     5.22    4.56     94
  1cam_1fr_1diff      yes   |  792 ms      1.26     1.28    4.44     23

  Takeaways:
  - CoC adds ~500–600ms of autoregressive VLM decoding per pred. Proportional hit is brutal on the small config (5.2 Hz → 1.3 Hz, −75%), modest on the heavy one (1.0 Hz → 0.67 Hz, −33%).
  - ADE didn't improve with CoC on this clip (4cam went 1.52→2.01, 1cam 4.56→4.44). Sample counts are small (12–94) so single-clip deltas are noisy — not a general claim about CoC
  quality.
  - Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769/ (no-CoC) and alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_withcot/ (CoC).

❯ great from now on we don't want to do CoC. What other ways could we make this run faster on the H100? It seems like to have decent minADE, we are going to need to run 4 cams, 4 frames, 
   and 4 diffusion steps. Make a table with expected speedup and quality degradation for each option. (give me the top 10 options)                                                         

⏺ Baseline to beat: 4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC on H100 ≈ ~310 ms gpu_inf (3.2 Hz). Dropping 10→4 diff steps already gives ~1.25× vs the 405 ms number you saw.

  Rough decomposition of that 310 ms (estimated, not profiled yet): vision ~160 ms, VLM prefill ~90 ms, expert diffusion @ 4 steps ~60 ms.

  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Reduce Qwen3-VL pixel budget                   │ 1.40–1.80× on vision → │ 5–20% (depends on        │        │ no (but RL/SFT   │ Fewer image tokens cuts ViT and shrinks  │
  │ 6   │ (min_pixels/max_pixels, e.g. 448² → 336²)      │  1.20–1.40× total      │ distant-obstacle detail) │ Low    │ would help       │ VLM prefill.                             │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │ regain)          │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ Eliminates per-forward kernel launch     │
  │ 7   │ CUDA graphs + static KV cache for VLM          │ 1.10–1.20× total       │ none                     │ Med    │ no               │ overhead. Works well once shapes are     │
  │     │                                                │                        │                          │        │                  │ pinned.                                  │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 8   │ Distill expert to fewer diffusion steps        │ 1.05–1.10× total       │ 3–10%                    │ High   │ yes              │ Diffusion is only ~20% of total at 4     │
  │     │ (consistency/LCM, 4→2)                         │                        │                          │        │                  │ steps — smaller leverage than it sounds. │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Vision-token reuse across adjacent ticks       │ 1.50–2.50× on vision → │ 3–10% (mostly on         │        │                  │ Practical on highway/straight driving;   │
  │ 9   │ (reuse t-1's ViT output for 3 of 4 cams when   │  1.30–1.60× total in   │ fast-changing scenes)    │ Med    │ no               │ disable during turns/intersections.      │
  │     │ egomotion < threshold)                         │ steady driving         │                          │        │                  │                                          │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 10  │ Distill VLM backbone to 3B (Cosmos-Reason2-8B  │ 2.0–2.5× total         │ 5–15%                    │ Very   │ yes              │ Biggest ceiling lift. Pays for itself on │
  │     │ → Qwen3-VL-3B or similar)                      │                        │                          │ High   │                  │  Thor where the VLM dominates.           │
  └─────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┴────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Stacking guidance (no retrain): 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 together are realistic to combine and should land ~1.8–2.3× total → ~140–170 ms gpu_inf → 6–7 Hz on H100 with ADE essentially unchanged.
  Add 6 (reduced pixels) if you can tolerate modest quality loss → push past 8 Hz.

  Caveat: these ranges are priors based on how Qwen3-VL-scale models typically respond to each intervention, not measurements on this model. The right next step is to actually profile the
   4cam/4fr/4diff no-CoC H100 run with a stage breakdown (we have vlm_ms / vision_ms / diff_ms timers already in benchmark()) so the ranking reflects real bottlenecks instead of
  estimates.

✻ Cooked for 1m 27s

※ recap: Deploying Alpamayo-R1-10B for real-time inference; just benchmarked 4cam/4fr/10diff no-CoC on H100 (2.5 Hz, ADE 1.56m) and delivered a top-10 speedup options table. Next: pick
  which optimizations to actually implement.

⏺ Done. Side-by-side on H100, no-CoC, clip 25cd4769:

  config           model           | gpu_inf   Hz     ADE(m)   n_preds
  4cam_4fr_10diff  Alpamayo-R1     |  405 ms  2.50   1.56      45
  4cam_4fr_10diff  Alpamayo-1.5    |  458 ms  2.22   1.22      40
  1cam_1fr_1diff   Alpamayo-R1     |   85 ms 11.72   4.62     211
  1cam_1fr_1diff   Alpamayo-1.5    |   92 ms 10.89   7.72     196

  Takeaways:
  - 4cam/4fr/10diff: Alpamayo-1.5 is ~13% slower but ~22% better ADE (1.22m vs 1.56m) — meaningful quality win.
  - 1cam/1fr/1diff: Alpamayo-1.5 is similar speed but 67% worse ADE (7.72m vs 4.62m). Likely because 1.5's prompt embeds explicit camera-name + frame-index annotations
  (_build_image_content in helper.py) — training assumes multi-cam; feeding a single front-wide is out-of-distribution.
  - Preprocess time jumped (335ms vs 303ms), and data_load went up too — the 1.5 processor uses min_pixels=163840 (it's the Qwen3-VL-2B processor), which causes larger image tokenization
  per frame.

  Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_alp15_h100/.

  Recommendation: if you pick Alpamayo-1.5, stick to 4-cam configs — the quality advantage only materializes when the input matches its training distribution.

  more flexibke camera setups                                                                                                                                                              

⏺ Looking at _build_image_content in alpamayo1_5/helper.py: it takes num_frames_per_camera (default 4) and uses it to expand camera_indices via repeat_interleave. Our build_messages never
   passes it — so for any config with n_frames ≠ 4, the prompt tells the model "you have 4 frames of this cam" when we actually passed fewer. That's likely contributing to the 1cam
  degradation.

  Let me fix the plumbing and add two richer 1-cam configs:

⏺ Background command "Run Alpamayo-1.5 1cam variants with fixed num_frames_per_camera" completed (exit code 0)                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                           
⏺ Results — Alpamayo-1.5 1-cam ablation on H100 (prompt format fixed, no CoC, clip 25cd4769):                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  config            | gpu_inf    Hz     ADE(m)    n_preds                                                                                                                                  
  1cam_1fr_1diff    |   93 ms   10.78   7.73      194     (fixed prompt, ≈ unchanged from 7.72m)                                                                                           
  1cam_4fr_4diff    |  182 ms    5.50   2.88       99     ← sweet spot                                                                                                                     
  1cam_4fr_10diff   |  338 ms    3.00   3.18       54                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Diagnosis:                                                                                                                                                                               
  - The prompt-format fix made no difference (7.73 ≈ 7.72). The num_frames_per_camera label wasn't the problem.
  - The actual problem was context starvation. Going from 1 frame → 4 frames dropped ADE from 7.73m → 2.88m (−63%) on the same 1-camera setup. 1.5's claim about "more flexible camera     
  setups" is true in the sense that 1-cam works well, as long as you give it 4 temporal frames.                                                                                            
  - More diffusion steps didn't help (2.88 → 3.18 going 4 → 10 steps; within noise given sample sizes 99 vs 54).                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Comparison at matched "realistic" settings:                                            
              model          cams×frames×diff  gpu_inf   Hz    ADE(m)                                                                                                                      
              Alpamayo-R1    4×4×10             405ms   2.50   1.56                                                                                                                        
              Alpamayo-1.5   4×4×10             458ms   2.22   1.22                                                                                                                        
              Alpamayo-1.5   1×4×4              182ms   5.50   2.88                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Takeaway for Thor deployment: if a single front-wide camera can give 2.88m ADE at ~5.5 Hz on H100, then Alpamayo-1.5 with 1cam_4fr is a viable "fast mode" that a multi-cam backup never 
  could be. Worth pushing the optimization stack (torch.compile / FP8 / FA3) on that config too — it has the most headroom. Videos at alpamayo_videos_25cd4769_alp15_1cam/.                
                                                                                                                                                                                           
※ recap: Goal: deploy Alpamayo for real-time inference on Jetson Thor. Just finished benchmarking Alpamayo-1.5 vs R1 on H100 with 1-cam ablations, finding 1cam_4fr_4diff hits 5.5 Hz at 
  2.88m ADE. Next: apply speedup optimizations (torch.compile, FP8, FA3) to 4cam_4fr_4diff.

  Key comparisons (no-CoC, H100):                                                                                                                                                          
  - Best ADE anywhere: Alpamayo-1.5 4cam_4fr_10diff → 1.22m @ 2.22 Hz                                                                                                                      
  - Best Hz with usable ADE: Alpamayo-1.5 1cam_4fr_4diff → 2.88m @ 5.5 Hz (singe front-wide cam, 4 temporal frames, 4 diff steps)                                                          
  - Alpamayo-1.5 vs R1 at 4×4×10: +22% better ADE (1.22 vs 1.56), −12% Hz (2.22 vs 2.50) — clear Pareto win when 4-cam rig is available                                                    
  - Alpamayo-1.5 vs R1 at 1×1×1: 1.5 is worse (7.73 vs 4.62m) because 1 frame starves temporal context; adding 3 more frames (1×4×4) recovers to 2.88m, beating R1's 4-cam 4.62m with fewer
   inputs                                                                                                                                                                                  
  - A100→H100 speedup, R1 no-CoC: 2.57× heavy / 2.25× light                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Video dirs: alpamayo_videos_25cd4769/ (A100 R1 no-CoC), _withcot/ (A100 R1 CoC), _h100/ (H100 R1), _alp15_h100/ (H100 1.5 4×4×10 + 1×1×1), _alp15_1cam/ (H100 1.5 three 1-cam variants).
